description_long: This object is a straight, narrow segment of a cycleway, typically found in urban environments for bicycle traffic. The surface is flat and dark, likely made of asphalt or concrete, with a subtle, slightly curved marking running along its length, possibly indicating a lane boundary. The edges are clean and slightly beveled, suggesting it is designed for smooth transitions with adjacent road or sidewalk surfaces. There are no visible barriers, signage, or bicycle symbols on the surface, indicating a minimalist design. The top view reveals a uniform, uninterrupted surface, while the side views show the thin profile of the cycleway. This segment is suitable for integration into a larger network of urban cycling infrastructure.
description: A straight, dark, flat segment of cycleway with a subtle lane marking and clean edges, designed for urban bicycle traffic. The surface is smooth and uninterrupted, lacking visible signage or symbols.
description_view_0: Front view shows the curved lane marking and the full width of the cycleway segment.
description_view_1: Side view reveals the thin, flat profile and length of the cycleway.
description_view_2: Rear view mirrors the front, showing the marking and width from the opposite side.
description_view_3: Opposite side view again highlights the thin, flat profile and length.
